title: Metrics Glossary Design Tweaks: Navigation, Group By, Create Metric, and Edit/Delete
story: As a DataTools Pro user managing metrics, I want an improved layout with navigation on top, a gear icon to edit taxonomies, a refined Create Metric form with a conditionally-enabled save button, and edit/delete functionality, so that I can manage my metrics efficiently.
description:
Multiple design tweaks to the Metrics Glossary page:

### Navigation
- Move navigation DIV to the top of the page

### Group By Section
- Replace filter-style select with a gear icon that toggles the taxonomy edit modal
- Gear icon opens the taxonomy management modal, pre-selecting the current taxonomy

### Create Metric Form
- Move save button below the input
- Save button shows as disabled by default
- Enable save button only when text is entered in the metric name field

### Table Group Styling
- Add custom styles to the table grouping

### Edit/Delete Metrics
- Implemented delete: removes metric and cleans up taxonomy junction table rows
- Implemented edit: uses DataTable Editor popup form with custom PDO backend (bypassing DataTable Editor's default controller)
acceptance_criteria: Navigation appears at the top of the page
Gear icon opens taxonomy edit modal with current selection
Create Metric save button is disabled until text is entered
Metrics can be edited via popup form
Metrics can be deleted with taxonomy junction cleanup
ux_changes: Reordered navigation to top. Replaced filter icon with gear icon for taxonomy editing. Refined Create Metric form with conditional save button. Added edit/delete metric functionality.
middleware_changes: Custom PDO-based edit handler bypassing DataTable Editor default controller. Delete handler cleans up taxonomy junction table rows.
data_model_changes: Taxonomy junction table cleanup on metric deletion.
implementation_notes: DataTable Editor's built-in controller was bypassed — only the frontend popup form is used, with custom PDO PHP code for the actual edit/delete operations. When deleting a metric, associated taxonomy junction rows are also removed to maintain data integrity.
comment_summary: Developer implemented navigation changes, Create Metric form tweaks, and delete functionality (including taxonomy junction cleanup). The gear icon initially caused confusion — it was clarified to open the taxonomy edit modal. Edit functionality was implemented using DataTable Editor's frontend form with a custom PDO backend. Reporter confirmed the final implementation looked good.
